No Poverty.reason: The course covers property law, contracts, and inheritance, directly aligning with Target 1.4 on equal rights to economic resources, ownership, and control over property and inheritance.
evidence: 物權法的一般規則 (General Rules and Property)

Zero Hunger.reason: The course content, focusing on Japanese civil law, does not contain any explicit or implicit references to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course discusses legal aspects of "fatal torts" and "issues related to the liability for children and elderly people in a shrinking-aging society," tangentially relating to health risks (Target 3.d).
evidence: 生命權侵害時損害賠償之繼承可能性 (the possibility of inheritance of damages in case of fatal torts)
少子高齡社會中幼童和高齡者侵權之問題 (issues related to the liability for children and elderly people in a shrinking-aging society)

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (4.3) and skills for legal careers (4.4). Its comparative law approach and critical analysis of legal systems foster knowledge for sustainable development and cultural diversity (4.7).
evidence: 本課程為日本民法入門的課程 (This course is an introduction to Japanese civil law)
有興趣於未來從事會使用日本法相關工作的同學 (students interested in careers involving Japanese law)

Gender Equality.reason: The course explicitly addresses "structural inequalities" in damage assessment for a "young female" and "same-sex common law marriage," aligning with ending discrimination and promoting legal equality (Targets 5.1, 5.c).
evidence: 損害賠償額計算中投影出的構造性不平等 (projection of structural inequalities in the assessment of damages)
年輕女孩的餘命損害 (Assessing lost profits for the life of a young female)
同性伴侶的事實上婚姻 (Same-sex common law marriage)

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: The course content, focusing on Japanese civil law, does not contain any explicit or implicit references to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course content, focusing on Japanese civil law, does not contain any explicit or implicit references to energy or related infrastructure.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course covers civil law concepts like property and contracts, which establish the foundational legal framework necessary for economic activity and decent job creation (Target 8.3).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course content, focusing on Japanese civil law, does not contain any explicit or implicit references to industry, innovation, or infrastructure development.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course directly addresses "structural inequalities" and discrimination through case studies on gender, sexuality ("Same-sex common law marriage"), and ethnicity, aligning with Targets 10.2 and 10.3.
evidence: 損害賠償額計算中投影出的構造性不平等 (projection of structural inequalities)
同性伴侶的事實上婚姻 (Same-sex common law marriage)
錯讀韓國人姓名事件 (Legal interest to have one’s name pronounced correctly)

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course content, focusing on Japanese civil law, does not contain any explicit or implicit references to urban development, housing, or community sustainability.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course content, focusing on Japanese civil law, does not contain any explicit or implicit references to consumption patterns, resource efficiency, or waste management.

Climate Action.reason: The course content, focusing on Japanese civil law, does not contain any explicit or implicit references to climate change or related actions.

Life Below Water.reason: The course content, focusing on Japanese civil law, does not contain any explicit or implicit references to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: The course content, focusing on Japanese civil law, does not contain any explicit or implicit references to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course is fundamentally about understanding Japanese civil law, legal provisions, and court decisions, directly promoting the rule of law and ensuring access to justice (Target 16.3).
evidence: 日本民法入門 (Introduction to Japanese Civil Law)
透過各種法律規定和實務判決 (through its legislation... and court decisions)

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course is an international academic partnership, co-taught by professors from different institutions, fostering knowledge sharing and international cooperation (Target 17.6).
evidence: co-taught by Daniel Machado, Associate Professor of Japanese civil law at Gakushuin University, and Assistant Professor Chang Yunchi
本課程由學習院大學Daniel Machado准教授、張韻琪助理教授共同授課
